intro:
There is no information available on a plant called "Frangula surotatensis." However, I can provide information on Frangula (also known as Cascara Sagrada or Rhamnus purshiana). 

Frangula is a plant species in the buckthorn family (Rhamnaceae), specifically known for its bark, which is traditionally used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Frangula include its potential to support digestive health, stimulate bowel movements, and relieve constipation due to its anthraquinone content. The bioactive constituents of Frangula include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Frangula include teas, tinctures, and capsules, often used to treat constipation, irritable bowel syndrome, and other gastrointestinal issues.
